content.problemStatement:
Given the schedule of Chef for 303030 days (A binary string of length 303030 where `'0'` denotes that Chef was on leave and `'1'` denotes Chef was working on that day). Chef gets X−X-X− Rs for every day he worked. As working continuously for a long time is hectic so Company introduced the following policy to give a bonus to its employees.

The company will check the longest streak for which an employee has worked and will award Y−Y-Y− Rs for every day of that streak as a bonus. Calculate the salary received by Chef (including the bonus).

**Note:** Rs represents the currency of Chefland, and if there are two or more longest streaks of the same length, only one is counted for the bonus.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The TTT test cases then follow.
The first line of each test case contains XXX and YYY.
Second line contains a binary string (i.e it contains only `‘0’` / `‘1’`), where `'0'` denotes that Chef was on leave and `'1'` denotes Chef was working on that day.


Output Format

For each testcase, output in a single line answer to the problem. i.e The salary received by Chef (including the bonus).
constraints: S consists only of characters '0' and '1'
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers X and Y (1 ≤ X, Y ≤ 999). The second line contains a binary string S of length 30, where '1' denotes a working day and '0' denotes a leave day.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the total salary Chef receives (base pay plus bonus).

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we count the number of working days (ones) and the length of the longest consecutive block of ones. Salary = (ones * X) + (longestStreak * Y). The three calculations give 117, 152 and 78 respectively.
No working days, so both base pay and bonus are zero.
All 30 days are working days. Base pay = 30*999, longest streak = 30, bonus = 30*999, total = 60*999 = 59940.

solution.approach: For each test case we scan the string once. While scanning we keep three counters: totalOnes (number of '1's), currentStreak (length of the current consecutive '1's), and maxStreak (maximum value of currentStreak seen so far). After the scan the salary is totalOnes * X + maxStreak * Y. The algorithm is O(|S|) per test case, which is O(30) ≈ O(1).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
